# Video Tutor - Future Scope and Roadmap

While the core functionality of Video Tutor—uploading videos, processing them with Gemini, and engaging in Q&A/Quizzes—is fully operational, there are several exciting avenues for future expansion to make this a truly comprehensive AI learning platform.

## 1. Live Translation and Multilingual Support
*   **Real-time Dubbing:** Utilize AI voice cloning and translation models to offer real-time or pre-processed audio dubbing of the original video into the user's native language.
*   **Interactive Subtitles:** Clickable, auto-generated subtitles that pause the video and provide instant dictionary definitions or contextual explanations.
*   **Multilingual Chat:** Allow users to ask questions in their native language, having the AI agent translate the context from the video and reply back in the user's language.

## 2. Advanced Learning Tools
*   **Automated Flashcards (Spaced Repetition):** Automatically extract key concepts, dates, and definitions from the Gemini summary to generate Anki-style flashcards for spaced repetition learning.
*   **Mind Map Generation:** Visually represent the core concepts of a video utilizing the transcript to automatically draw interactive mind maps or concept webs.
*   **Progress Tracking & Analytics:** Monitor a user's quiz scores, watch time, and interaction depth to provide personalized learning paths and recommendations.

## 3. Collaboration and Workspaces
*   **Shared Notebooks:** Allow multiple users to take collaborative, time-stamped notes on the same video simultaneously.
*   **Group Study Rooms:** Watch parties where the AI acts as a moderator or tutor for a group of students discussing a lecture.

## 4. Enhanced Content Ingestion
*   **Live Stream Ingestion:** Connect to live Twitch or YouTube streams, allowing users to ask questions about the ongoing broadcast in real-time.
*   **Document Context (PDFs/Slides):** Allow users to upload supplementary materials (like a professor's slide deck) alongside the video, so the AI can cross-reference the spoken lecture against the written slides.

## 5. Export and Integrations
*   **Export to Notion/Obsidian:** Provide one-click exports of summaries, Q&A logs, and generated quizzes directly into popular note-taking applications.
*   **LMS Integration:** Integrate with systems like Canvas or Blackboard so educators can instantly turn their lecture recordings into interactive, graded assignments.
